demographic_audit_note: Audited face sheet against Atlantis; corrected zip code from 19082 to 19083. Verified Medicare Advantage Plan coverage for post-acute home health. DOB and phone number confirmed with spouse.
home_vs_ltc_determination: Social Worker (SW) confirms the discharge goal is Home with HHA; patient has strong family support and no clinical indicators for LTC placement.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: Patient is meeting PT goals for ambulation. SW reports a 'Caregiver Training Gap' as the daughter is struggling to attend sessions for post-surgical incision monitoring and drain care.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card saved to patient's mobile device; physical program flyer delivered to the spouse and daughter during the education session.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached daughter via phone; she confirmed the final training session with the facility nurse was completed. Home is prepared, and she understands the HHA start-of-care window.
atlantis_final_sync: Entered D/C date of 11/14 and first visit date of 11/15 into the Atlantis system.
ma_visit_booking: MA visit scheduled for 11/15 at 11:30 AM; confirmed with HHA intake that the RN start-of-care is booked for the same morning to avoid gap.
narrative_summary: Patient was triaged in Atlantis following cardiac bypass; PN audited demographics and confirmed the home discharge goal with the SW. During the maintenance phase, a caregiver training gap delayed the timeline by four days, which the PN tracked via weekly check-ins while educating the family on the HHA's role in bridging the care gap. The transition concluded with a successful 24-hour pulse call, Atlantis data synchronization, and scheduling the MA for the first visit within the required 24-hour window.

format_2_triples.situation: Daughter expresses anxiety about managing the cardiac drain at home.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Educated daughter on the 'Day 1' HHA expectations, including the nurse's role in verifying drain care.

format_2_triples.situation: SW is unavailable to confirm if the daughter's training session was rescheduled.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Contacted the facility floor nurse to verify if the training was logged in the chart without accessing the EMR directly.

format_2_triples.situation: Conflicting information regarding the HHA's ability to staff a cardiac case in this zip code.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Escalated the staffing concern to the SW to ensure an alternative agency was identified if the primary HHA could not commit.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The PN monitors the SW's progress on the caregiver training schedule and waits for a confirmed discharge date before initiating the pre-discharge pulse call.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Respects the SW boundary regarding discharge readiness while ensuring Stage 3 actions only occur once the clinical barrier is resolved by the facility.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The PN walks the family through the specific checklist of equipment and supplies they need to have ready for the HHA's first visit, while checking with the SW to see if the training gap has been closed.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Follows the Stage 2 checklist for patient education and stakeholder alignment without crossing into clinical instruction or discharge planning.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The PN observes the daughter struggling with the drain and offers to provide a hands-on demonstration of the emptying and measurement procedure to ensure she is ready for a Friday discharge.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Directly violates the Banned Actions by managing clinical tasks and performing medical education that is the sole responsibility of the facility nursing staff.
